headline: Seychelles first-baseline rotation assessment. Small Island Developing State (SIDS), ~100,000 population, established-band composite. No active news in May 2026 recency window. Strong democratic transitions baseline (2020 peaceful power transfer). Climate-change vulnerability is a notable EQU/SYS context but not in immediate window. Existing dimension means consistent with established-band SIDS baseline. Math-hygiene flag: published composite 63.9 cannot reconstruct from current dimension means (reconstructed 60.9). Recommend confirm + math-hygiene flag.
key_evidence: Stable democratic transitions: 2020 peaceful presidential transfer (first since independence).
Commonwealth and African Union member state.
SIDS climate-vulnerability framework engagement; UNEP partnerships.
Universal healthcare; high HDI ranking among African states.
No active news in May 2026 recency window.

evidence.finding: Member state in good standing; democratic governance.

scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.0 confirmed. Income-distribution and climate-vulnerability concerns consistent with SIDS profile.

math_check.validator_check: Math-hygiene discrepancy noted. No composite change proposed.

math_hygiene_flag.issue: Published composite 63.9 vs reconstructed 60.9. Discrepancy +3.0 points.

positiveSignals: 2020 peaceful power transfer; Commonwealth/AU membership; Freedom House Free designation.
sector_context: Small Island Developing States cluster. Climate-vulnerability and economic-resilience are dominant SYS concerns for SIDS — outside scoring window for this baseline cycle.
